rulings.Case Title: ROBERTO BARRAGAN VS. COMMUNITY VISION CAPITAL & CONSULTING, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Mar-27-2025, line 1, DEFENDANTS PATRICIA GOPAUL, CATHERINE HOWARD, ROSS CULVERWELL, C. LEA SALEM, JOY HOFFMAN, DAVID ELSSAESER, JIM SNYDER, ANITA ADDISON, CAROLYN JOHNSON, PAMELA MERCHANT, ANDY MADEIRA, JOHN CHAN, LOUISA MEDINA, BYRAN IGNOZZI, AND COMMUNITY VISION CAPITAL & CONSULTING'S Notice Of Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. The unopposed petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JANE GAZZOLA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of The Person Most Qualified Of Defendant And Cross-Defendant Wilad Properties, Llc And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Mar-27-2025, line 8, 1-DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of The Person Most Qualified Of Defendant And Cross-Defendant Wilad Properties, Llc And Request For Sanctions. CCP SEC. 2025.450(a) provides, "If, after service of a deposition notice, a party to the action or an officer, director, managing agent, or employee of a party, or a person designated by an organization that is a party under Section 2025.230, without having served a valid objection under Section 2025.410, fails to appear for examination, or to proceed with it, or to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice, the party giving the notice may move for an order compelling the deponent's attendance and testimony, and the production for inspection of any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice." CCP SEC. 2025.450 requires the Court to compel the deposition unless it finds a valid objection was served under SEC. 2025.410. Defendant Wilad Properties LLC ("Wilad") concedes the noticed February 20, 2025 deposition did not occur and argues a timely objection was served. Defendant City and County of San Francisco contends that Defendant Wilad's objection was untimely as it was not personally served as required by CCP 2025.410(b). Both parties accuse the other side of failing to meet and confer. The deposition has yet to occur more than a month after the missed date, and it was originally noticed on December 17, 2024. (Schroeder Dec. para. 6.) The motion to compel is GRANTED. (CCP SEC. 2025.450(a).) Defendant Wilad's person most qualified is ordered to appear for deposition at a date, time, and location to be noticed by Defendant no later than April 4, 2025 unless the parties mutually agree to a later date. Defendant Wilad shall serve code compliant responses to the document requests set forth in the Amended Notice of Deposition and produce any responsive documents. CCP SEC. 2025.450(g)(1) requires the Court to impose sanctions unless it finds the deponent acted with substantial justification or there are circumstances that render imposition of sanctions unjust. The court does not find substantial justification in Wilad's failure to timely provide its deponent even considering Wilad's sur-reply's recitation of its meet and confer and scheduling efforts. Sanctions are appropriate. Defendant City and County of San Francisco requests $6,148.50 in sanctions for the cost of the nonappearance and motion filing. The court does not find this amount reasonable and reduces it. Wilad must pay sanctions of $2,500 to Defendant City and County of San Francisco, no later than April 14, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CREDIT CORP SOLUTIONS INC., VS. JASON JONES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reconsider Defendant'S Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Mar-27-2025, line 2, DEFENDANT JASON JONES' Motion To Reconsider Defendant's Motion To Dismiss. Defendant moves this court to reconsider its February 14, 2025 order that took his motion to dismiss off calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies. Local Rule 2.7(B)(5) provides that "Failure to lodge courtesy copies of moving Papers as required by statute, rule or court order may, in the discretion of the judicial officer presiding over the hearing, result in denial of the motion, continuance of the hearing, or taking the motion off calendar." In this case, the court took the matter off calendar, which means no decision was reached on the motion. Defendant filed a supplemental notice of motion on March 7 recognizing the above and asking the court to consider the motion to dismiss as a new filing (but nonetheless not providing courtesy copies of the moving papers on the motion to dismiss). The supplemental notice was not timely filed. (CCP 1005(b) [moving papers must be served and filed at least 16 court days before hearing].) Plaintiff did not respond to the supplemental notice or the merits of the motion to dismiss but instead argued that there was no basis for reconsideration. The court denies the relief requested by Defendant-an order of dismissal-but notes that Defendant may refile his motion to dismiss as a new motion and may obtain a hearing by complying with Local Rule 2.7(B). The court's procedural rules help it to manage its large caseload; they may seem onerous to individual litigants but they serve the efficient administration of justice across the run of cases. Defendant also requests that the court bifurcate his cross-complaint to permit him to proceed on the cross-complaint separately. The court denies this request without prejudice to any determination by the trial judge to bifurcate. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Limiting The Number Of Requests For Admission And Specially Prepared Interrogatories; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Chance Pardon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Mar-27-2025, line 9, PLAINTIFF NEREIDA CRUZ', AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion For A Protective Order Limiting The Number Of Requests For Admission And Specially Prepared Interrogatories. Plaintiff's motion for a protective order limiting the number of special interrogatories and requests for admission is DENIED as moot. In support of the opposition, defense counsel Christopher Maloney declares that Defendants withdrew special interrogatories (SPROGs) and requests for admission (RFAs) so that each Defendant is propounding no more than the statutory floor. This moots the issues raised in the motion. To the extent Plaintiff wanted the Court to limit all three Defendants to 35 cumulative requests/interrogatories, that request is denied. Each party can propound 35 RFAs/SPROGs. (CCP Section 2033.030; CCP Section 2030.030(a)(1).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: JACOB HARKER ET AL VS. JOEL FINK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, Mar-27-2025, line 3, 1-DEFENDANT JOEL FINK'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Fink's Motion to Strike is GRANTED in part, and DENIED in part. Defendant Fink's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Defendant Fink argues all allegations that he illegally held himself out to be a private investigator must be stricken because of the federal injunction. This is unpersuasive. The injunction does not squarely resolve whether these allegations are false. The injunction prevents enforcement by the California DOJ, and acknowledges a finding that Fink was "likely to succeed in showing that, as applied to him, California's private investigator licensing requirement fails rational basis review." (Ex. B. to RJN, at 2, internal citation omitted.) In any event Fink does not show that a final judgment has been rendered and has not made any argument that it is preclusive as to claims by these plaintiffs. Next, Plaintiffs admit in opposition that the prayer for damages is improper on these causes of action. Thus, the unopposed motion to strike the prayer for damages is granted. However, Plaintiffs have shown that the complaint is capable of amendment by adding a cause of action for concealing stolen property. Thus, leave to amend the FAC is granted. Plaintiffs have 20 days from the date of this order to file their Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiffs shall prepare the order for the court's endorsement. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
